publishedAbstract: - Beginning with OpenShift Data Foundation 4.19, the ODF architecture implements a hub-spoke model (also referred to as provider/managed clusters). In this model, a central cluster (the **hub**) acts as a storage **provider** to one or more **managed** clusters. The OCS storage client operator runs on a **consumer (managed)** cluster and connects it to the external OCS/ODF storage provider (the hub).- For the internal deployment model provided by Red Hat, only **one** StorageClient is supported: the one used by the hub cluster for self-communication. StorageClients on separate OpenShift clusters (external spokes) are not supported.- This document describes how the OCS storage client operator and the StorageClient resources work.
publishedTitle: Red Hat Openshift Data Foundation: Understanding the StorageClient CR and OCS Client Operator (ODF 4.19+)
